article_meta.title: PlanetOur Almost-Apocalyptic Climate FutureBy shooting for 3 degrees Celsius of warming, the world could slide toward a

reasoning: The summary adopts much of the article’s emotive framing (e.g., “fails to meaningfully,” “catastrophic,” “mass migration of billions,” “disastrous”) without attribution, rather than using neutral phrasing. It also introduces an inaccurate/unattributed claim (“32,000 people weekly”). Perspectives are under-represented, and sources_represented is incomplete.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses interpretive phrasing like “fails to meaningfully reduce” and “trigger catastrophic consequences” rather than neutral alternatives.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: States “32,000 people weekly” without support and presents sweeping outcomes (billions migrating) with minimal attribution.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Frames the COP outcome as a failure and portrays predicted impacts as settled outcomes without clear attribution.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Relies on dramatic terms (catastrophic, deadly, breakdown) mirroring the source’s tone.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: Does not infer intentions or thoughts.
custom_reasoning.slant: Emphasizes shortcomings and dire projections; limited acknowledgment of counter-frames (e.g., climate pragmatism argument).
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Omits the ‘climate realism/pragmatism’ perspective beyond a brief nod to ‘some progress’; leaves out quoted voices and alternative frames present in the article.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Some charged wording; mostly standard climate terminology otherwise.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Strong focus on negative outcomes; includes a brief mention of improved forecasts.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No clear deference to elites or dismissal of grassroots perspectives.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Replicates the article’s dramatic framing rather than neutralizing it.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Misses several perspectives (e.g., climate pragmatism advocates, UN officials, scientific uncertainty nuances) and lists only ‘The Atlantic’ as source.
